472	\begin{lemma}\leanok
473	[Affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
474	  \label{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
475	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
476	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso,
477	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars,
478	        lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt,
479	        lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
480	% NOTE: the carrier-wall obstruction to `hloc` is resolved (restriction-of-scalars /
481	% scalar-tower instances feed lem:powers_restrictScalars; lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule
482	% supplies the R'-side localization). The single remaining obligation is the open-naturality of
483	% the section comparison e_{D(a)}, established as a prose remark in the proof of
484	% lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt; the proof sketch below derives each per-a hloc(a) from the top-level
485	% localization plus that naturality square for the inclusion D(a) ⊆ ⊤, so no section-level square
486	% is hand-proved per a.
487	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
488	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
489	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
490	  \[
491	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\,\widetilde{M}
492	    \;\cong\;
493	    \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)},
494	  \]
495	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
496	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
497	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
498	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) is the \(R\)-module obtained from \(M\) by
499	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). That is, pushing the quasi-coherent
500	  sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) forward along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to
501	  this canonical identification, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of \(M\).
502	
503	  This is the classical fact that the direct image of a quasi-coherent sheaf along
504	  a morphism of affine schemes is computed on modules by restriction of scalars; it
505	  is the affine companion of the pullback formula (the affine case of the
506	  pushforward analogue of Stacks ``$\widetilde{M}$ and pullback''). It is recorded
507	  here as bespoke project infrastructure because the affine-pushforward
508	  identification does not appear as a ready-made result in the existing
509	  quasi-coherent sheaf theory.
510	\end{lemma}
511	
512	\begin{proof}
513	  \uses{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis, lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional, lem:powers_restrictScalars, lem:gammaPushforwardIso, lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt, lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
514	  We build the isomorphism \emph{directly on a basis of basic opens}. This is the
515	  non-circular route: checking the comparison map on basic opens yields the object
516	  isomorphism, and quasi-coherence of the pushforward is then a corollary, not a
517	  prerequisite. (One must not reverse this order: the global-sections comparison
518	  \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{M}\bigr) \cong
519	  \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) of
520	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso} alone would only upgrade to an object
521	  isomorphism once the pushforward is already known to be quasi-coherent, so
522	  quasi-coherence cannot be deduced from an object isomorphism built that way.)
523	
524	  \emph{The comparison morphism.} There is a canonical morphism of
525	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
526	  \[
527	    \alpha : \widetilde{\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)}
528	    \longrightarrow \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M},
529	  \]
530	  namely the tilde-adjoint of the global-sections identification of
531	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}. We show \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
532	
533	  \medskip
534	  \emph{Reduction to basic opens.} The basic opens \(D(a)\), for \(a \in R\), form a
535	  basis of the topology of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). By the basis-local criterion
536	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_of_isBasis} it suffices to check that \(\alpha\)
537	  restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over each basic open \(D(a)\).
538	
539	  \medskip
540	  \emph{The computation over \(D(a)\).} On the source, the sections of
541	  \(\widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) over \(D(a)\) are the
542	  localization \(\bigl(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\bigr)[1/a]\) as an
543	  \(R[1/a]\)-module. On the target, since \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) =
544	  D(\varphi a)\), the sections of \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*
545	  \widetilde{M}\) over \(D(a)\) are the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over
546	  \(D(\varphi a)\), i.e.\ the localization \(M[1/\varphi a]\), regarded as an
547	  \(R[1/a]\)-module by restriction of scalars along the induced map
548	  \(R[1/a] \to R'[1/\varphi a]\). These two localizations agree: the element \(a\)
549	  acts on \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) through \(\varphi a\), so localizing
550	  \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at the multiplicative set generated by \(a\) is
551	  the same module as localizing \(M\) at the multiplicative set generated by
552	  \(\varphi a\). Both are the universal localization of
553	  \(M\) inverting \(\varphi a\), so the comparison map \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism
554	  on the sections over \(D(a)\). As this holds for every basic open, \(\alpha\) is
555	  an isomorphism.
556	
557	  \medskip
558	  \emph{The explicit formalization route over \(D(a)\).} The agreement of the two
559	  localizations just described is the entire content, but it must be realized
560	  through the global ring \(R\), because the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
561	  sections over \(D(a)\) is built from the global-sections module of
562	  \(N := (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M\) rather than directly from
563	  \(R[1/a]\). The reduction therefore proceeds exactly as the conditional
564	  assembly Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} prescribes: it
565	  suffices to supply, for each \(a \in R\), the hypothesis \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) that
566	  the structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) exhibits
567	  \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) as the localization of \(\Gamma(N, \top)\) at
568	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\). The \(R\)-action on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) is the honest
569	  restriction-of-scalars action carried by the scalar tower \(R \to R' \to {-}\)
570	  determined by \(\varphi\); against that structure the ring-change converse
571	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applicable, so the carrier of the
572	  \(R\)-module is exactly the one to which the localization claim refers, and no
573	  recursively-defined or ad-hoc section-level scalar action intervenes.
574	  We obtain \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transporting the already-known \(R'\)-side
575	  localization (Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}), in
576	  element-free fashion, across the open-indexed section comparison of
577	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} \emph{using its naturality in the open}
578	  (the naturality remark in the proof of
579	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}). This proceeds in three
580	  movements.
581	
582	  \emph{(1) The \(D(a)\)-level linear equivalence.} This is the component at
583	  \(U = D(a)\) of the open-indexed family of
584	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the \(R\)-linear isomorphism
585	  \[
586	    e_{D(a)} : \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
587	      \;\cong\;
588	      \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr) ,
589	  \]
590	  using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
591	  definitional. No fresh \(D(a)\)-level construction is needed: this is precisely the
592	  open-indexed isomorphism already supplied by
593	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} evaluated at the basic open \(D(a)\).
594	
595	  \emph{(2) The naturality square for \(D(a) \subseteq \top\).} The compatibility
596	  that relates \(e_{D(a)}\) to the \(\top\)-level isomorphism
597	  \(e_\top\) (the global-sections comparison of
598	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) is precisely the open-naturality square of the
599	  family \(\{e_U\}_U\) (established in the proof of
600	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}), instantiated at the inclusion
601	  \(D(a) \subseteq \top\): the square
602	  \[
603	    \begin{array}{ccc}
604	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, \top\bigr)
605	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_\top\;}
606	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top)\bigr) \\[4pt]
607	      {\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}}\big\downarrow & &
608	        \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}} \\[4pt]
609	      \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde M,\, D(a)\bigr)
610	        & \xrightarrow{\;e_{D(a)}\;}
611	        & \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(\varphi a))\bigr)
612	    \end{array}
613	  \]
614	  commutes, using \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}D(a) = D(\varphi a)\), which is
615	  definitional. Because the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) is a natural isomorphism, this single
616	  square is available uniformly in \(a\); there is no per-\(a\) section-level identity
617	  to re-prove by hand. It intertwines the source restriction
618	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) on the pushforward with the (restriction-of-scalars
619	  reading of the) target restriction \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)}\) on
620	  \(\widetilde M\).
621	
622	  \emph{(3) Discharging \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) by transport.} The target restriction
623	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(\varphi a)} : \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
624	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(\varphi a)) =
625	  M[1/\varphi a]\) as the \(\mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)-localization of
626	  \(M = \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) over \(R'\); this is exactly
627	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}. Apply the ring-change converse
628	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} (with \(R\)-algebra \(A = R'\) and submonoid
629	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\), so that
630	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_{R'}(\mathrm{powers}(a)) = \mathrm{powers}(\varphi a)\)) —
631	  its \(\operatorname{Algebra} R\, R'\) and scalar-tower hypotheses being supplied by
632	  the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\) — to read this same map as
633	  the \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization over \(R\). The commuting naturality square
634	  of movement (2) intertwines this target restriction with the source restriction
635	  \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)} : \Gamma(N, \top) \to \Gamma(N, D(a))\) by way of the
636	  isomorphisms \(e_\top\) and \(e_{D(a)}\); transporting the localization property
637	  across that square — via the transport-along-an-equivalence principle for localized
638	  modules — yields that \(\mathrm{res}_{\top \to D(a)}\) is the
639	  \(\mathrm{powers}(a)\)-localization on \(\Gamma(N, D(a))\) over \(R\), i.e.\
640	  \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\). No section-level computation specific to the individual \(a\)
641	  is performed: the only \(a\)-dependent input is the instantiation of the single
642	  natural-isomorphism square at \(D(a)\). Feeding the family
643	  \(\{\mathrm{hloc}(a)\}_{a \in R}\) to the conditional assembly
644	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_conditional} produces the unconditional
645	  object isomorphism \(\alpha\).
646	
647	  \medskip
648	  \emph{Naturality in the open drives the transport.} The comparison assembled
649	  here, \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)} \cong
650	  \widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\), is natural in \emph{both}
651	  arguments — in the module \(M\) and in the open \(U\). The naturality in the
652	  module is the usual functoriality; the naturality in the open is the naturality
653	  remark in the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}: the family
654	  \(\{e_U\}_U\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt} commutes with the
655	  structure-sheaf restriction maps on both sides. It is precisely this
656	  open-naturality that drives the localization transport \emph{uniformly}: the
657	  per-\(a\) localization fact \(\mathrm{hloc}(a)\) follows from the \(\top\)-level
658	  localization Lemma~\ref{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule} together with that
659	  naturality square for the inclusion
660	  \(D(a) \hookrightarrow \top\), rather than from a section-level naturality square
661	  re-proved by hand for each individual \(a\). This is the single remaining
662	  obligation of the construction; the carrier of the \(R\)-action on the pushforward
663	  sections is the restriction-of-scalars structure along \(\varphi\), against which
664	  Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} applies directly.
665	
666	  The \(R\)-module structure on the \(R'\)-side sections to which the ring-change
667	  converse Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is applied is the honest
668	  restriction-of-scalars structure: \(R\) acts through \(\varphi\), i.e.\ via the
669	  \(R\)-algebra structure on \(R'\) determined by \(\varphi\) and the associated
670	  scalar tower \(R \to R' \to M[1/\varphi a]\). It is \emph{not} an ad-hoc,
671	  recursively-defined scalar action. Lemma~\ref{lem:powers_restrictScalars} is
672	  stated against exactly this restriction-of-scalars / scalar-tower structure, and
673	  it is under that structure that localizing \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\) at
674	  \(a\) coincides with localizing \(M\) at \(\varphi a\).
675	
676	  % NOTE: This lemma is the affine case of the general fact that the pushforward of
677	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf along an affine morphism is quasi-coherent. In current
678	  % Mathlib master that fact is the IsLocalizing / fromTildeGamma characterization
679	  % (AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward); it post-dates the project's
680	  % pinned Mathlib, so the project proves it in-tree by the basic-open route above.
681	  % The in-tree proof remains valid and is not made redundant by a future Mathlib
682	  % bump.
683	  \medskip
684	  \emph{Quasi-coherence as a corollary.} The object isomorphism just constructed
685	  exhibits \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M} \cong
686	  \widetilde{(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M)}\) as the tilde of a module. Tilde
687	  modules are quasi-coherent and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules
688	  is closed under isomorphism, so the pushforward
689	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. This
690	  conclusion is stated \emph{after} the object isomorphism, of which it is a
691	  consequence — no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' input is
692	  needed for the affine lane.
693	\end{proof}
694	
695	\begin{remark}
696	  This single isomorphism discharges both inputs that the affine reduction below
697	  requires of the pushforward of a tilde-module.
698	  \begin{enumerate}
699	    \item \emph{The global-sections comparison.} Applying the global-sections
700	      (module-of-sections) functor to the isomorphism identifies the
701	      \(R\)-module of sections of
702	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) with
703	      \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi M\); this is exactly the \(\Gamma\)-fragment
704	      comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map.
705	    \item \emph{Quasi-coherence of the pushforward.} The tilde of any module is
706	      quasi-coherent, and quasi-coherence of \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\)-modules is
707	      closed under isomorphism; hence
708	      \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)_*\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent.
709	      In particular no separate ``pushforward preserves quasi-coherent'' theorem is
710	      needed for the affine lane.
711	  \end{enumerate}
712	  Because both inputs the affine reduction asks of the pushforward are corollaries
713	  of this single object isomorphism — the section-level module identification and
714	  the quasi-coherence of the pushforward — this lemma is the sole bespoke
715	  ingredient the affine lane requires.
716	\end{remark}

718	\subsection{The pullback companion}
719	
720	The affine reduction below requires, alongside the pushforward dictionary, the
721	\emph{pullback} companion: the affine description of the inverse image of a
722	tilde-module. It is the affine case of the classical fact that the pullback of a
723	quasi-coherent sheaf is computed on modules by base change of scalars.
724	
725	The pullback companion is built by uniqueness of left adjoints from the
726	per-object \(\Gamma\)-fragment comparison of
727	Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, packaged as a natural isomorphism of
728	right-adjoint functors (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}).
729	
730	\begin{lemma}
731	
732	\leanok
733	[Naturality packaging of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison]
734	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
735	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardNatIso}
736	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
737	  The per-object comparison of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} assembles into a
738	  natural isomorphism of the two right-adjoint functors
739	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \circ \widetilde{(-)}\) and
740	  \(\widetilde{(-)} \circ \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\) on modules, exhibiting the
741	  pushforward dictionary as a natural transformation rather than merely a family of
742	  object isomorphisms. This is the input to the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints argument
743	  that produces the pullback companion Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}.
744	\end{lemma}
745	\begin{proof}
746	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
747	  The components are the object isomorphisms of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso};
748	  naturality is the corresponding naturality square, which holds because every
749	  constituent of the comparison is identity-on-elements.
750	\end{proof}
751	
752	\begin{lemma}
753	
754	\leanok
755	[Affine pullback of a tilde-module]
756	  \label{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
757	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
758	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
759	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma `lemma-widetilde-pullback', Tag 01I9
760	  % (\S\,Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex,
761	  % L1241--1256).
762	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let
763	  % $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(S), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(S)})$,
764	  % $(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
765	  % be affine schemes.
766	  % Let $\psi : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
767	  % morphism of affine schemes, corresponding to the ring map
768	  % $\psi^\sharp : R \to S$ (see Lemma \ref{lemma-category-affine-schemes}).
769	  % \begin{enumerate}
770	  % \item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
771	  % functorially in the $R$-module $M$.
772	  % \item We have $\psi_* \widetilde N = \widetilde{N_R}$ functorially
773	  % in the $S$-module $N$.
774	  % \end{enumerate}"
775	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Lemma ``\(\widetilde M\) and pullback''
776	  (Tag 01I9), part (1).}
777	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
778	  be an \(R\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
779	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-modules
780	  \[
781	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\,\widetilde{M}
782	    \;\cong\;
783	    \widetilde{\bigl(R' \otimes_R M\bigr)},
784	  \]
785	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
786	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
787	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
788	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) is the base change of \(M\) along \(\varphi\) — the
789	  \(R'\)-module obtained by extension of scalars. The isomorphism is natural in
790	  \(M\). That is, pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) back along
791	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to this canonical identification, the tilde
792	  of the base change of \(M\). It is the pullback companion of the affine
793	  pushforward dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}, and is part~(1)
794	  of the same Stacks lemma of which the pushforward formula is part~(2).
795	\end{lemma}
796	
797	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The first assertion follows from the identification in
798	% Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}
799	% and the result of Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-restrict-quasi-coherent}."
800	\begin{proof}
801	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
802	  Pullback along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, on quasi-coherent sheaves, the
803	  extension-of-scalars operation \(- \otimes_R R'\) on modules transported through
804	  the \(\widetilde{(-)}\) / \(\operatorname{moduleSpec}\Gamma\) equivalence between
805	  modules and quasi-coherent sheaves. Concretely, the global sections of
806	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\widetilde{M}\) are the base change
807	  \(R' \otimes_R M\): the pullback functor \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{*}\) is
808	  left adjoint to the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\), and under
809	  the global-sections identification the latter is restriction of scalars along
810	  \(\varphi\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}); the left adjoint of
811	  restriction of scalars is extension of scalars \(M \mapsto R' \otimes_R M\). The
812	  comparison map is the unit of the base-change adjunction, and the universal
813	  property of base change identifies it with the canonical isomorphism
814	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\widetilde{M} \cong
815	  \widetilde{(R' \otimes_R M)}\). Naturality in \(M\) is the naturality of that
816	  unit. (One may equally read this off the standard-open description: on
817	  \(D(\varphi a)\) the localization \((R' \otimes_R M)[1/a]\) is
818	  \(R'[1/\varphi a] \otimes_{R[1/a]} M[1/a]\), which is the base change of the
819	  sections of \(\widetilde M\) over \(D(a)\).)
820	\end{proof}

822	\subsection{The affine base-change lemma and its remaining obligations}
823	
824	The affine base-change lemma below proceeds \emph{directly on global sections}: it
825	reduces, via the locality criterion
826	Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} and the two affine dictionaries
827	Lemmas~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso},
828	to two obligations that are isolated here as named blocks. Both are extracted from
829	the proof of Stacks ``Affine base change'', and both are absent from the pinned
830	Mathlib. The first is the \emph{locality reduction}: restricting to the affine opens
831	of \(S'\) reduces the isomorphism claim to the affine--affine model
832	\(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\), \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\),
833	\(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\). The second is the
834	\emph{section-level identification}: in that affine--affine model, the global-sections
835	incarnation \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) of the base-change map, transported through the two
836	proved tilde dictionaries, is the canonical cancellation isomorphism
837	\(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) — an isomorphism with no flatness hypothesis. This
838	is the honest cut of the affine close: locality on \(S'\), then a pure computation on
839	the module of global sections. No separate abstract adjoint-mate identification is
840	performed at the level of sheaves; the mate is unwound \emph{on sections}, where it
841	becomes \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) directly.

844	
845	\leanok
846	[Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map]
847	  \label{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}
848	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_map_affine_local}
849	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
850	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
851	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
852	  % "local on $S$ and $S'$" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
853	  % L920--926).
854	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
855	  % assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
856	  % $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
857	  % for some $A$-module $M$."

859	  change'' (proof, local-on-\(S\)-and-\(S'\) step).}
860	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
861	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, with a base-change square as above. The formation of the
862	  base-change map (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is compatible
863	  with restriction to affine opens of \(S'\): for each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\), the
864	  restriction of the base-change map to sections over \(U\) agrees with the
865	  base-change map of the square obtained by restricting along \(U\). Consequently,
866	  by the affine-open locality criterion
867	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
868	  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
869	  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
870	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
871	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).
872	
873	\end{lemma}
874	
875	\begin{proof}
876	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
877	  By the affine-open locality criterion
878	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the base-change map is an isomorphism
879	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules as soon as its restriction to the sections over each
880	  affine open \(U \subseteq S'\) is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
881	  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
882	  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
883	  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
884	  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
885	  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
886	  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.
887	
888	  \medskip
889	  \emph{Step 1: unfold the map.} By
890	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, the base-change map is the image,
891	  under the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose — the natural bijection
892	  \(\operatorname{Hom}(g^*A, B) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(A, g_*B)\) — of the morphism
893	  \[
894	    f_*\mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\,f_*(\eta_{\mathcal{F}})\,}
895	    f_*(g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F} = g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F},
896	  \]
897	  where \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}} : \mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the unit of
898	  the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction and the identification
899	  \(f_*(g')_* = g_*\,f'_*\) is the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward applied to the
900	  square's commutativity \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\). Thus the map is, by
901	  construction, the transpose of the \(f_*\)-image of a unit — a composite of
902	  adjunction data and pushforward functors, with no ingredient beyond these.
903	
904	  \medskip
905	  \emph{Step 2: restriction to \(U\) commutes with each building block.} Fix an affine
906	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) and write \(\rho_U\) for restriction to \(U\) —
907	  equivalently, the section functor \(\Gamma(U, -)\) on \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules,
908	  or pullback along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow S'\). Restriction along
909	  \(U\) is a functor, and every ingredient of Step 1 is a natural transformation or a
910	  natural bijection; so applying \(\rho_U\) to the transpose-of-a-unit of Step 1
911	  returns the transpose-of-the-unit built from the restricted data. Concretely:
912	  \begin{itemize}
913	    \item[(a)] the unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}}\) of the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction
914	      restricts to the unit of the corresponding adjunction on the restricted square,
915	      because units are natural in their object and pulling the cartesian square back
916	      along \(U \hookrightarrow S'\) yields again a cartesian square (with total space
917	      the open \((f')^{-1}(U)\) of \(X'\));
918	    \item[(b)] \(f_*\) of a restricted morphism is the restriction of \(f_*\) of the
919	      morphism, because pushforward commutes with restriction to an open of the base:
920	      for an open \(U\) of the base one has
921	      \((f_*\mathcal{G})|_U = (f|_{f^{-1}U})_*\bigl(\mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}U}\bigr)\),
922	      naturally in \(\mathcal{G}\) — and likewise for \((g')_*\) and \(f'_*\);
923	    \item[(c)] the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose is a bijection natural in both
924	      variables, so it commutes with restricting its source and target along \(U\).
925	  \end{itemize}
926	  Chaining (a), (b), (c) — i.e.\ transporting the transpose-of-unit composite of
927	  Step 1 through the restriction functor \(\rho_U\) and replacing each block by its
928	  restricted counterpart — shows that the restriction of the base-change map to
929	  sections over \(U\) is itself the base-change map formed from the cartesian square
930	  restricted over \(U\). Choosing an affine open
931	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing the image \(g(U)\), affineness of
932	  \(f\) (local on the base and stable under base change) makes the total space
933	  restrict to an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), and quasi-coherence of
934	  \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict to a tilde-module \(\widetilde{M}\) there; so this
935	  restricted map is exactly the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out
936	  over \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). The whole
937	  identification is thus definitional plus the naturalities (a)--(c); it introduces no
938	  obligation beyond them.
939	
940	  \medskip
941	  \emph{Step 3: conclude the reduction.} By Step 2 the per-affine-open hypothesis
942	  required by the locality criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} —
943	  that the base-change map restricts to an isomorphism on sections over each affine
944	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) — is, under the identification of Step 2, precisely the
945	  affine--affine section assertion. That assertion is supplied, with \emph{no} flatness
946	  hypothesis, by the section-level computation
947	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, which identifies the
948	  affine--affine base-change map on global sections with the cancellation isomorphism
949	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Applying the locality criterion
950	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} to this family of per-open

951	  isomorphisms shows that the base-change map is an isomorphism, as asserted.
952	\end{proof}

954	\subsection{The section-level base-change identity}
955	
956	The section-level identification
957	Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
958	highest-leverage obligation of the affine close, and we prove it \emph{directly on
959	global sections}, with no abstract adjoint-mate tower. In the affine--affine model the
960	base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
961	proved tilde dictionaries, exactly the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of
962	extension of scalars; post-composing with the canonical regrouping isomorphism
963	\((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \cong R' \otimes_R M\) identifies it with the canonical
964	base-change comparison, an isomorphism carrying no flatness hypothesis. The computation
965	factors through three named reads — the domain read, the codomain read, and the single
966	\emph{section-level identity} that the conjugated map is the base change of the unit —
967	together with the one Mathlib-absent crux identifying the pullback cone legs with the
968	\(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the tensor inclusions
969	(Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}). Two Mathlib results are recorded
970	first as dependency anchors.

1197	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
1198	  inclusions. Reading the codomain \(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\) along the \emph{explicit composite}
1199	  legs \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\),
1200	  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R'\)-modules
1201	  \[
1202	    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat} :
1203	    \Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)
1204	    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1205	    \operatorname{restr}_{\iota_{R'}}\bigl(\operatorname{ext}_{\iota_A} M\bigr)
1206	    \;=\; (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M ,
1207	  \]
1208	  assembled as the composite-adjunction comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\)
1209	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) of the two free legs \(e\) and
1210	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) — the square's commutativity entering through the pushforward
1211	  congruence \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\) and the pushforward composition coherence
1212	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\) — followed by the affine pullback dictionary
1213	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) along \(\iota_A\) and the affine pushforward dictionary
1214	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) along \(\iota_{R'}\). Because the comparison object is
1215	  built from the free morphisms with \emph{no} leg-equality proof carried, its conjugate inverts to
1216	  the right-adjoint comparison in one step
1217	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}); this is what frees the downstream
1218	  reindex coherence from any transport across a propositional leg-equality. Instantiating at the
1219	  literal projection legs \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) recovers
1220	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
1221	\end{lemma}
1222	
1223	\begin{proof}
1224	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
1225	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}
1226	  The construction is the assembly described in the statement, and it carries no equality-of-legs
1227	  data, so there is nothing to transport. The composite-adjunction comparison
1228	  \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) packages the two
1229	  free legs \(e\), \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) together with the pasted square's pushforward
1230	  coherences (\(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\), \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\)) into a single
1231	  right-adjoint-read isomorphism; composing it with the two affine dictionaries

1233	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat}\). Its agreement with
1234	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} at the literal projection legs is the cone-leg
1235	  factorization.

1238	% --- Supporting lemmas for the codomain read ---
1239	% These helpers establish that pullback along a scheme isomorphism is an equivalence
1240	% of module categories; the adjunction unit of an equivalence is an isomorphism,
1241	% used in the codomain-read computation.
1242	
1243	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1244	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1245	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1247	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1248	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1249	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1250	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1251	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1252	\end{lemma}
1253	\begin{proof}
1254	
1255	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1256	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1257	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1258	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1259	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1260	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1261	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1262	\end{proof}
1263	
1264	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1265	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1266	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1267	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1268	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1269	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1270	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1271	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1272	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1273	\end{lemma}
1274	\begin{proof}
1275	
1276	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1277	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1278	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1279	\end{proof}

1281	% --- Section-level identity, three pieces: regroup equiv (pure tensor algebra, native
1282	%     R'-linear via Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange) ← section identity (Γ(θ) is the
1283	%     R'-base change of the algebraic unit) ← IsIso corollary (the pinned leaf). ---
1284	
1285	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1286	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1287	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1289	  % NOTE: tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1290	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1291	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1292	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1293	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1294	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1295	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1296	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1297	  % L933--938).
1298	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1299	  % equality
1300	  % $$
1301	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1302	  % $$
1303	  % as $R'$-modules."
1304	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1305	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1306	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1307	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1308	  \[
1309	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1310	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1311	  \]
1312	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1313	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1314	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1315	  hypothesis.
1316	\end{lemma}
1317	
1318	\begin{proof}
1319	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1320	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1321	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the natively \(R'\)-linear
1322	  pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1323	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1324	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1325	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1326	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1327	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1328	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1329	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1330	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1331	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1332	
1333	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1334	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1335	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1336	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1337	  dictionaries,
1338	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1339	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1340	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1341	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1342	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1343	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1344	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1345	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1346	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1347	  \[
1348	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1349	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1350	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1351	  \]
1352	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1353	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1354	
1355	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1356	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1357	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1358	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1359	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1360	\end{proof}

1366	%       (3) lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose — the (g^* ⊣ g_*) transpose on sections.

1368	\begin{lemma}
1369	
1370	\leanok
1371	[The affine pullback--pushforward unit is the algebraic unit]
1372	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}
1373	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_unit_value}
1374	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
1375	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
1376	  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
1377	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L932--937).
1378	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
1379	  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
1380	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
1381	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1382	  Let \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) be the canonical inclusion
1383	  (\(a \mapsto a \otimes 1\)). The unit of the
1384	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*, (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\)-adjunction
1385	  evaluated at the affine sheaf \(\widetilde M\), read on the global sections over
1386	  \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) through the tilde dictionaries
1387	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) and
1388	  the tilde--\(\Gamma\) unit \(\widetilde{(-)}\Gamma\)-iso, equals the algebraic unit of the
1389	  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\iota_A\), namely
1390	  \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1391	\end{lemma}
1392	\begin{proof}
1393	  \uses{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
1394	  This is the affine, square-free heart of the computation: there is no pullback square and
1395	  no base-change map yet, only the comparison of two units of two adjunctions related by the
1396	  two tilde dictionaries. The geometric adjunction
1397	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^* \dashv (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\) on
1398	  sheaves of modules transports, under the tilde dictionaries which identify
1399	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*\) of a tilde with extension of scalars
1400	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) and \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\) of a tilde
1401	  with restriction of scalars (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}), to the algebraic
1402	  extension/restriction adjunction \((\iota_A^{\,*} \dashv \iota_{A\,*})\) on
1403	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\). The dictionaries being the comparison isomorphisms of these
1404	  adjunctions, naturality identifies the two units; evaluating at \(M\) gives the stated
1405	  value \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1406	\end{proof}

1580	\begin{lemma}
1581	
1582	\leanok
1583	[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]
1584	  \label{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1585	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1586	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1587	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1588	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to X_3\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
1589	  \(N\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_3}\)-module. Write \(\eta^{h}\) for the unit of the
1590	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \((h^{*}\dashv h_{*})\) attached to a morphism \(h\), and
1591	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b}\) for the pseudofunctor
1592	  composition coherences. Then
1593	  \[
1594	    \eta^{b}_{N} \;\circ\; b_{*}\bigl(\eta^{a}_{\,b^{*}N}\bigr)
1595	      \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{N}
1596	    \;=\;
1597	    \eta^{a\circ b}_{N} \;\circ\;
1598	      (a\circ b)_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_{N}\bigr),
1599	  \]
1600	  where composites are read left to right. In words: the unit of the composite morphism
1601	  \(a \circ b\), reindexed by the inverse pullback coherence, factors as the \(b\)-unit, followed
1602	  by \(b_{*}\) of the \(a\)-unit, followed by the pushforward coherence. This is the leg-reindex
1603	  engine consumed by Seam~2 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}): with \(a\) an
1604	  isomorphism and \(b\) an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map it turns the generic projection-leg
1605	  unit into the affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1606	\end{lemma}
1607	\begin{proof}
1608	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1609	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1610	  Apply the unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} to the
1611	  composite adjunction \((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}) . (a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\), the adjunction
1612	  \(\bigl((a\circ b)^{*}\dashv (a\circ b)_{*}\bigr)\), and the left-adjoint transformation
1613	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\). This yields an identity whose right-hand factor
1614	  is the conjugate (mate) of \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\); by
1615	  Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} that mate is exactly the pushforward
1616	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}\). Expanding the unit of the composite
1617	  adjunction by Lemma~\ref{lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib} —
1618	  \(\bigl((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}).(a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\bigr)\text{-unit}_N
1619	    = \eta^{b}_N \circ b_{*}(\eta^{a}_{b^{*}N})\) — and reassociating the resulting composite gives
1620	  the stated equation.
1621	\end{proof}

2016	  rewrite of the locked section composite.

2018	  \emph{Conjugate reformulation.} The codomain comparison is assembled, by
2019	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}, as the composite-adjunction comparison
2020	  \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) of the two free
2021	  legs \(e\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), carrying no leg-equality data. Both sides of the
2022	  desired identity are values of the conjugation bijection
2023	  \(\operatorname{conj} = \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_L,\mathrm{adj}_R)\)
2024	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib}); since that bijection is injective, it suffices to prove
2025	  the equality of their conjugate preimages on the right-adjoint side.
2026	
2027	  \emph{Lifting the locked transformations to free variables.} Each natural transformation appearing
2028	  on the right-adjoint side is, by surjectivity of the conjugation bijection, the conjugate of a free
2029	  left-adjoint-side transformation; replacing each by such a free preimage removes every
2030	  diamond-locked literal from the goal. The remaining identity is then a formal consequence of the
2031	  coherence of the conjugation operation.
2032	
2033	  \emph{Discharge by the conjugate coherences.} The conjugate of the inverse composite comparison is
2034	  the right-adjoint isomorphism \(e\) in one step
2035	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}); the conjugate of the inverse
2036	  pullback coherence is the pushforward coherence
2037	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}); the conjugation-composition,
2038	  conjugation-whiskering and conjugation-associator coherences fuse the per-adjunction conjugates of
2039	  \(\mathrm{adj}_L\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_R\) (the conjugate-side form of the paste-of-mates); and on the
2040	  pushforward side the transparent composition and congruence coherences collapse on sections
2041	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id},
2042	  \ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}, \ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}).
2043

2045	  pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison \(\gamma_\psi\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) — enters as
2046	  the transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib},
2047	  composed one functor layer up through the conjugation-composition coherence (the Seam~1 tool, raised
2048	  one layer), rather than as a positional naturality rewrite. The lone surviving affine
2049	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit evaluates by Seam~1
2050	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) to \(\rho\)
2051	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}).

2092	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,

2096	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs} at the literal pullback projection legs
2097	  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\): with the codomain read
2098	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} being definitionally the variable-legs read at
2099	  those legs, the concrete statement follows directly. The remainder of this proof records the
2100	  mathematical content of that abstract identity.
2101	
2102	  Throughout, let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the
2103	  canonical isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}, and
2104	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
2105	  inclusions, so that by Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} the two projection legs
2106	  factor as
2107	  \[
2108	    g' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A,
2109	    \qquad
2110	    f' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'} .
2111	  \]
2112	  The inner composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the four-factor map
2113	  \[
2114	    f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr) \;\circ\;
2115	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g', f})^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2116	    (\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2117	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f', g})^{\mathrm{inv}},
2118	  \]
2119	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
2120	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
2121	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
2122	
2123	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
2124	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
2125	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
2126	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
2127	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
2128	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
2129	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
2130	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
2131	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
2132	
2133	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
2134	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2135	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2136	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2137	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2138	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2139	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2140	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2141	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2142	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2143	
2144	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2145	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2146	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2147	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2148	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2149	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2150	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2151	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2152	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2153	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2154	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2155	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2156	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2157	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2158	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2159	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2160	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2161	  sections.
2162	
2163	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
2164	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
2165	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
2166	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
2167	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
2168	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
2169	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
2170	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
2171	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
2172	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
2173	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
2174	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
2175	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
2176	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
2177	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
2178	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
2179	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
2180	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
2181	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
2182	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
2183	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
2184	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
2185	\end{proof}

2638	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2639	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2640	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2641	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2642	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2643	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2644	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2645	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2646	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2647	  \[
2648	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2649	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2650	  \]

2652	\begin{proof}
2653	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2654	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2655	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2656	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2657	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2658	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2659	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2660	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2661	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2662	\end{proof}

3214	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
3215	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
3216	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.
3217	
3218	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
3219	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
3220	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
3221	  \mathlibok
3222	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3223	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
3224	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
3225	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
3226	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
3227	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
3228	  \[
3229	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
3230	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
3231	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
3232	  \]
3233	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
3234	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
3235	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
3236	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
3237	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
3238	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
3239	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
3240	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
3241	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
3242	\end{lemma}
3243	
3244	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf condition as an equalizer of products]
3245	  \label{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}
3246	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts}
3247	  \mathlibok
3248	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3249	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf with values in a category with products on a
3250	  topological space, and let \(\mathcal{U} = \{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of
3251	  opens with union \(U = \bigcup_i U_i\). Then \(\mathcal{F}\) satisfies the sheaf
3252	  condition on \(\mathcal{U}\) if and only if the fork
3253	  \[
3254	    \mathcal{F}(U)
3255	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\mathrm{res}\;}\;
3256	    \prod_i \mathcal{F}(U_i)
3257	    \;\underset{\mathrm{rightRes}}{\overset{\mathrm{leftRes}}{\rightrightarrows}}\;
3258	    \prod_{(i,j)} \mathcal{F}(U_i \cap U_j)
3259	  \]
3260	  is a limit (an equalizer), where the two parallel maps restrict the \(U_i\)- and
3261	  \(U_j\)-components to \(U_i \cap U_j\). This is Mathlib's
3262	  \texttt{isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafEqualizerProducts} together with the fork
3263	  \texttt{SheafConditionEqualizerProducts.fork}; it is exactly Čech degree
3264	  \(0 \to 1\) and requires no separatedness.
3265	\end{lemma}
3266	
3267	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3268	[Finite affine cover with quasi-compact overlaps]
3269	  \label{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3270	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact}
3271	  A quasi-compact scheme \(X\) admits a \emph{finite} affine open cover
3272	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\). If moreover \(X\) is
3273	  quasi-separated, then each pairwise intersection \(U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j\) is
3274	  quasi-compact; and if \(X\) is separated, each \(U_{ij}\) is in fact affine.
3275	  \begin{proof}
3276	    The affine opens form a basis, so \(X\) is covered by affine opens; quasi-compactness
3277	    extracts a finite subcover \(U_1, \dots, U_t\) (by Mathlib's affine cover and the
3278	    finite-subcover property of a quasi-compact space). Quasi-separatedness says the
3279	    intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact, so each \(U_{ij}\) is
3280	    quasi-compact. When \(X\) is separated the diagonal is a closed immersion, whence
3281	    the intersection of two affine opens is affine (Mathlib, for separated schemes).
3282	  \end{proof}
3283	\end{lemma}
3284	
3285	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3286	[$\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})$ as a finite sheaf-condition equalizer]
3287	  \label{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer}
3288	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork}
3289	  % NOTE (iter-033): the pinned Lean decl is strictly more general than this prose — it takes ANY
3290	  % open family `U : ι → X.Opens` over ANY scheme (no QCQS / finite / affine hypotheses), giving
3291	  % `IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U)`. The finite affine cover of a QCQS scheme is the intended
3292	  % application; the consolidated existential `Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork`
3293	  % now has its own block, Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}.
3294	  \uses{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3295	  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a sheaf of
3296	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Fix a finite affine cover \(\mathcal{U} = \{U_i\}_{1 \le i \le t}\)
3297	  of \(X\) as in Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}. Then the module of global
3298	  sections is the equalizer of the two restriction maps of the cover,
3299	  \[
3300	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
3301	    \;=\;
3302	    \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
3303	      \textstyle\prod_{i} \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F})
3304	      \;\rightrightarrows\;
3305	      \textstyle\prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})
3306	    \Bigr),
3307	    \qquad
3308	    s \mapsto (s_i|_{U_{ij}}), \quad s \mapsto (s_j|_{U_{ij}}),
3309	  \]
3310	  and the products are \emph{finite} (indexed by the finite sets \(\{i\}\) and
3311	  \(\{(i,j)\}\)).
3312	  \begin{proof}
3313	    \uses{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3314	    Apply the equalizer-products form of the sheaf condition
3315	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}) to the underlying sheaf of
3316	    \(\mathcal{F}\) and the cover \(\mathcal{U}\): since \(\bigcup_i U_i = X\), the
3317	    fork displayed there computes \(\mathcal{F}(X) = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) as the
3318	    equalizer of \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows \prod_{i,j}
3319	    \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\). The cover is finite by
3320	    Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}, so both products are finite. The
3321	    module structure is inherited objectwise: the forgetful functor to abelian
3322	    groups preserves and reflects the limit, so the equalizer is computed in
3323	    \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) over the ground ring.
3324	  \end{proof}
3325	\end{lemma}

3327	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3328	[Global sections as a finite equalizer over a finite affine cover]
3329	  \label{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
3330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork}

3333	  \emph{exists} a finite affine open cover \(\{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) of \(X\) — \(\iota\) finite, each
3334	  \(U_i\) affine, \(\bigsqcup_i U_i = \top\), all pairwise intersections \(U_i \cap U_j\)
3335	  quasi-compact — whose sheaf-condition fork of the underlying presheaf of \(M\) is a limit:
3336	  \[
3337	    \Gamma(X, M) \;=\; \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
3338	      \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, M)
3339	    \Bigr).
3340	  \]

3342	  sheaf-condition equalizer Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} into a single existential, the
3343	  directly-usable ``global sections as a finite equalizer'' input of the flat-base-change argument.
3344	  \begin{proof}

3347	    Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}, and apply the sheaf-condition equalizer
3348	    Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} (in its form valid for any open family) to that cover; the
3349	    pair witnesses the existential.
3350	  \end{proof}

3353	\subsection{The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear equalizer presentation (FBC-B build-ahead)}
3354	
3355	The separated-case flat base change Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated} can be organised by
3356	presenting \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\) as an honest \(\operatorname{LinearMap.eqLocus}\) over the global
3357	ring \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and invoking the Mathlib base-change-of-equalizer equivalence
3358	\(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\) (recorded as the dependency anchor
3359	Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}, which pins exactly
3360	\texttt{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}). This sub-lane is independent of the affine section-level
3361	computation; its three ingredients are recorded here as build-ahead blocks. The Lean pins below name
3362	to-be-built declarations.
3363	
3364	\begin{lemma}[$A$-module structure on cover sections by restriction of scalars]
3365	  \label{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}
3366	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaCoverRestrictScalars}
3367	  \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
3368	  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme, \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\), and write
3369	  \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) for the ring of global sections of the structure sheaf. For a finite
3370	  affine cover \(\{U_i\}\) with overlaps \(U_{ij}\), the structure-sheaf restriction ring maps
3371	  \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and
3372	  \(A \to \Gamma(U_{ij},\mathcal{O}_X)\) make each module of sections \(\Gamma(U_i, M)\),
3373	  \(\Gamma(U_{ij}, M)\) an \(A\)-module by restriction of scalars; moreover the restriction maps of
3374	  \(M\) along inclusions of opens are \(\Gamma(X,-)\)-semilinear over those restriction ring maps,
3375	  hence \(A\)-linear after restriction of scalars.
3376	  \begin{proof}
3377	    \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
3378	    Each \(\Gamma(U_i,M)\) is a module over \(\Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\); restricting scalars along
3379	    the ring map \(A \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) gives the \(A\)-module structure, and likewise on
3380	    \(U_{ij}\). A presheaf restriction of \(M\) over an inclusion \(V \subseteq U\) is additive and
3381	    semilinear over the corresponding restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\); since both the
3382	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)- and \(M\)-restrictions are compatible with the maps out of the global sections
3383	    \(A\), the restriction becomes \(A\)-linear once scalars are restricted to \(A\).
3384	  \end{proof}
3385	\end{lemma}
3386

3392	  the sheaf-condition fork,

3395	      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, M),
3396	  \]
3397	  (the maps \(\mathrm{SheafConditionEqualizerProducts.leftRes}/\mathrm{rightRes}\)) are \(A\)-linear,
3398	  i.e.\ read as morphisms in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat} A\) under the restriction-of-scalars

3409	\begin{lemma}[$\Gamma(X,M)$ as an $A$-linear equalizer locus]
3410	  \label{lem:gamma_eqLocus_iso}

3414	  \[

3520	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change, Mayer--Vietoris reduction of the quasi-separated case]
3521	  \label{lem:flat_base_change_mayer_vietoris}
3522	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_mayerVietoris}
3523	  \uses{lem:flat_base_change_separated, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3524	        lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3525	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH ($i=0$ case), quasi-separated paragraph
3526	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.md; quoted verbatim in the
3527	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF of thm:flat_base_change_pushforward below).
3528	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In case $X$ is quasi-separated, choose an affine open
3529	  % covering $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$. ... The reader who
3530	  % wishes to avoid this spectral sequence can use Mayer-Vietoris and induction
3531	  % on $t$ as in the proof of Lemma
3532	  % \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}."
3533	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), $i=0$ case,
3534	  quasi-separated paragraph (Mayer--Vietoris induction on \(t\)).}
3535	  Let \(A \to B\) be flat, \(X\) a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme over \(A\),
3536	  and \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent. Then the comparison map
3537	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to \Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\) is an
3538	  isomorphism.
3539	  \begin{proof}
3540	    \uses{lem:flat_base_change_separated, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3541	          lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3542	    Choose a finite affine cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\)
3543	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}) and induct on \(t\). For \(t = 1\),
3544	    \(X = U_1\) is affine and the claim is the affine lemma
3545	    Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}. For \(t > 1\) put \(V = U_t\)
3546	    (affine) and \(W = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_{t-1}\) (covered by \(t-1\) affines, hence
3547	    quasi-compact and quasi-separated). Their intersection \(W \cap V =
3548	    \bigcup_{i<t}(U_i \cap U_t)\) is covered by the \(t-1\) opens \(U_i \cap U_t\), each
3549	    an open of the affine \(U_i\) and so quasi-compact and separated; thus \(W \cap V\)
3550	    is separated and quasi-compact, and flat base change holds for it by the separated
3551	    case Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated}. The two-member cover
